title: Oracle v. PeopleSoft: Don't Wait Until the Bout is Over
author: David Alschuler and Tim Minahan
source_file: 2003 Oracle v. PeopleSoft_ Don_t Wait Until the Bout is Over.pdf
abstract: Aberdeen Group examines Oracle's hostile takeover pursuit of PeopleSoft in its third month as of August 2003, framing it as a competitive prizefight with both parties absorbing damage. The study advises PeopleSoft customers and prospects not to wait for the battle's resolution, implying a strategic posture for ERP acquisition decisions amid uncertainty. It poses the core question of whether PeopleSoft can survive as an independent software vendor.
importance_rationale: Published during the live hostile takeover battle in August 2003, this InSight captures a pivotal inflection point in enterprise software history when customers faced genuine uncertainty about the viability of a major ERP vendor. Aberdeen's framing of customer action during M&A uncertainty was directly actionable guidance.
relevance_rationale: The hostile-takeover framing and ERP customer decision framework remain highly applicable to modern enterprise software M&A scenarios. Specific PeopleSoft product details are dated, but the strategic guidance for customers during vendor uncertainty transfers to contemporary SaaS consolidation waves.
prescience_rationale: The study implicitly predicted continued market pressure on PeopleSoft and urged customers to act rather than wait — Oracle did complete the $10.3B acquisition in December 2004, vindicating both the urgency and the eventual outcome. The prediction that PeopleSoft's independence was at risk proved fully correct.
